danger_signals.visual_form: Animated projectiles from dinosaur and creature sprites
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat requiring dodge or defensive action

danger_signals.visual_form: Body of water with lilypads in stage environment
danger_signals.meaning: Instant death if player falls in; environmental danger state

danger_signals.visual_form: Platform elements that shift position or disappear beneath surface
danger_signals.meaning: Platforming challenge indicating loss of safe ground

danger_signals.visual_form: Large distinct enemy sprite at end of stage
Prehistoric creature sprites distinct from player character
danger_signals.meaning: Primary hostile agents in prehistoric setting
Loss of weapon/speed upgrade level; degraded combat capability

safety_signals.visual_form: Destructible terrain elements scattered throughout stages
safety_signals.meaning: Reward containers—destroying grants health and power-ups

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual emergence of weapon upgrade or mobility item from broken boulder
safety_signals.meaning: Combat enhancement; weapon progression from rocks → axes → boomerangs → torches
Mobility enhancement enabling hazard avoidance (water crossing)
Death respawn point; player returns here rather than stage start

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Primitive human character sprite with weapon (rocks/axes/boomerangs/torches)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Macho Riki transformation—muscular version firing self-clones (Riki Lariat attack); variant differs between JP (Riki Choshu likeness) and US (Takahashi Meijin/Master Higgins likeness)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player occupies foreground of scrolling stages; central to screen positioning

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Variety of dinosaur and prehistoric beast designs; each stage features thematic enemy roster
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Populate all stages; primary hostile presence in world

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Non-hostile but lethal; water is uniform kill-hazard regardless of stage
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Water and platforming sections occur in Stage 1; represents environmental threat distinct from creature enemies

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dinosaurs, primitive weapons (rocks, axes, torches), natural landscape with water hazards, platforming terrain
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player uses melee/thrown weapons; movement limited to jumping and directional locomotion; no technology-based tools

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player sprite at base weapon level (rocks); minimal projectile spread
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Reduced attack range and damage output; lower combat efficacy against enemies

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enhanced weapon sprite (axes/boomerangs/torches with wider visual spread)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased projectile count, range, and area coverage; improved enemy engagement capability

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character sprite changes to muscular variant (JP: Riki Choshu, US: Master Higgins); fires clones of self across screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Temporary power state with enhanced attack; fires projectiles of player sprite rather than standard weapons; time-limited duration

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Large distinct sprite at stage endpoint; arena-scale positioning
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Combat focused on single large threat; requires sustained attack and evasion of boss projectiles

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Low health indicator at top of screen; accumulated damage visual state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Single hit will kill; all power-ups stripped; reset to checkpoint on death

environmental_storytelling.example: Dinosaurs, primitive human protagonist, stone/bone weapons, natural terrain (water, lilypads, rocky landscape) establish 'stone age' world context without narrative exposition
Stage 1 features water/lilypad platforming section; subsequent stages implied to present different prehistoric environments and creature rosters

environmental_storytelling.technique: Hazard placement as implicit danger zones
environmental_storytelling.example: Water bodies positioned as instant-death zones telegraphs environmental lethality; lilypads as traversal puzzle communicate stage design intent
Large distinct enemy at stage end implies 'guardian' or 'apex threat' of that region; visual scale difference signals climactic encounter
Boulders containing upgrades suggest resource gathering and environmental interaction; rewards exploration and destruction

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Throws rocks in spread pattern; moves in all directions; can jump; baseline speed and range
Throws upgraded projectiles with increased range and spread; higher damage output
Can fly across water hazards and elevated terrain; enables vertical movement freedom
Fires clones of player sprite (Riki Lariat attack); temporary enhanced state; high damage output
Emit projectiles; move toward or away from player; distinct sprite patterns per type
Fires projectiles; occupies large screen area; requires focused combat; defeat triggers stage completion
Some move back-and-forth horizontally; others submerge and resurface in timed pattern; contact with water = instant death
Unbroken: visual terrain obstacle. Broken (hit by player): drops power-up item; visual reward manifestation

notes: Dino Riki is a vertically-scrolling shmup with minimalist visual communication. World-state is expressed primarily through enemy roster, weapon visual progression, and environmental hazards rather than explicit UI elements (aside from health/score/lives display at top). The game's core manifestation pattern revolves around weapon upgrade visualization—each tier (rocks → axes → boomerangs → torches) is immediately visually distinct, allowing player to assess combat capability at a glance. The Macho Riki transformation serves as a temporary power-fantasy state with distinct visual identity (character sprite change) and attack pattern (self-cloning projectiles), communicating 'ultimate power' through both visual and behavioral change. Danger is signaled through enemy projectiles, water hazards, and boss presence; safety through power-up collectibles hidden in destructible terrain. The prehistoric setting is communicated entirely through aesthetic—no narrative exposition required. Regional differences in Macho Riki sprite (Riki Choshu vs. Master Higgins) indicate localization adaptation but do not alter mechanical or world-state communication.
